human_verification.notes: Verified awareness and training specialty boundary, authoritative source coverage, source lineage expectations, metric/reporting caveats, output fidelity, refusal rules, escalation behavior, tenant-specific adaptation, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

source_classes_covered: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

review_notes: All 18 scenarios pass with explicit awareness, training, metric, reporting, caveat, refusal, escalation, and tenant-specific behavior.
All required abilities across shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and cross-industry layers are fully covered.

specialist_owned_evidence: Awareness outputs are tied to named LMS, phishing simulation, reporting-channel, HRIS, identity, policy, GRC, incident, risk, and evidence-store records.

The lane defines metrics and reporting caveats for completion, timeliness, report rate, susceptibility indicators, coaching closure, and role-based readiness without unsupported publication certainty.

The lane refuses live phishing execution, exploitation, malware analysis, incident response, formal audit attestation, legal compliance opinions, policy approval, employment decisions, and architecture ownership.
Tenant industry context materially changes records, cadence, caveats, privacy handling, and approval routing.
Runtime remains specification-based; retained generated implementation requires separate frequent-use promotion evidence.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after constraining jurisdiction, source-precedence, causal-claim, and publication-authority uncertainty to runtime blockers or escalation triggers.
Pack is suitable for validated spec-pack deployment and on-demand materialization. It can be promoted to a retained implementation only through a separate explicit build decision.
